displayText.simplePara: AMPEL360e Wide Tube-and-Wing Family (eWTW) — All variants

remarks.simplePara: Programme-controlled placeholder. infoCode 040A subject to BREX/SNS/DMRL freeze. Not for operational use.

levelledPara.title: General
levelledPara.para: This data module describes the Pressurization Control System of the AMPEL360e eWTW aircraft, aligned to ATLAS node 021-030 and ATA 21-30. The pressurization control system maintains safe cabin pressure throughout all phases of flight by regulating outflow, managing safety relief and controlling cabin altitude rate of change.

levelledPara.title: Pressurization Control Architecture
levelledPara.para: The pressurization control system uses an automatic closed-loop control architecture with manual override capability:

listItem.para: Cabin Pressure Controllers (CPC) — dual redundant, with automatic changeover.
Outflow valves — modulating valves controlling cabin pressure differential.
Safety relief valves — positive and negative pressure differential protection.
Negative pressure relief valves — prevent structural loading on descent.
Manual dump valves — crew-operated for emergency depressurization.

levelledPara.title: Control Logic
levelledPara.para: The pressurization control law schedules cabin altitude as a function of aircraft altitude, maintaining the cabin altitude below the maximum certified limit throughout the flight envelope. The control law also limits the rate of cabin altitude change to passenger comfort limits during climb and descent.

levelledPara.title: Safety and Airworthiness Boundary
levelledPara.para: Pressurization control is a safety-critical function. Loss of pressurization control can result in a hazardous cabin environment. The system is designed to applicable airworthiness standards. Failure condition classifications are determined by the system safety assessment and are subject to certification authority review. This data module is descriptive and does not constitute safety evidence or airworthiness approval.
